question.text: What is MF Central, and who operates it?

answer.text: A regulatory portal operated by SEBI for mutual fund compliance
A mobile app operated by AMFI for NAV information
A government platform for tracking mutual fund taxes
A common transaction platform operated jointly by CAMS and KFintech for all mutual fund transactions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** MF Central is a common digital platform jointly operated by the two major RTAs — CAMS (Computer Age Management Services) and KFintech (formerly Karvy/KFIN Technologies). It provides investors a single platform to execute transactions, view holdings, and manage their mutual fund investments across ALL AMCs, regardless of which RTA services them. **The Trap:** MF Central is NOT a regulatory or government platform — it's an industry initiative by the two RTAs. It's also different from MFU (Mutual Fund Utility) which was an earlier attempt at a common platform. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series II-B specifically tests MF Central as a key service infrastructure; it represents the evolution of RTA services beyond back-office processing to direct investor servicing. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Direct tech-savvy clients to MF Central (mfcentral.com) for self-service transactions across all AMCs. It reduces dependence on individual AMC platforms and provides consolidated access. For clients who prefer human assistance, you remain the interface — but knowing about MF Central adds to your credibility.
